attacking_style: Reactive counter-attacker who waits for opponents to move first, then responds inside final 500m-1km. Reads premature accelerations and exploits rivals' timing errors on summit finishes and in breakaways.
team_tactics: Typically operates with tactical freedom in breakaways rather than traditional domestique role. Has shown willingness to attack mid-race for team tactical goals, though primary value is individual opportunism in reduced groups.
terrain_preference: Summit finishes and reduced bunch scenarios where positioning and timing matter more than pure power. Thrives in tactical break sprints where he can sit on wheels before countering.
vulnerability: Lacks pure finishing speed against stronger sprinters when counter-attack is neutralized (Bilbao example). Mid-race aggression attempts have failed to create meaningful splits in larger pelotons.
key_patterns: Counter-punches inside final 500m rather than initiating, documented in both 2023 wins (Dauphiné, TDF stage).
Reads opponents' premature accelerations and exploits timing errors, successful twice in final kilometer scenarios.
Operates in breakaways with finishing freedom rather than pure domestique duties across different race types.
Attempts tactical mid-race attacks for team purposes but lacks power to sustain decisive splits alone.
form_trajectory: positive: secured first WorldTour win in June 2023 and immediately followed with strong TDF counterattack showing, indicating peak tactical confidence
